title: ornithogalum squilla
intro: Ornithogalum squilla, also known as Squill, is a bulbous perennial plant in the Asparagaceae family, native to the Mediterranean region and parts of Europe. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which make it useful for treating respiratory issues and skin conditions. The health benefits of Ornithogalum squilla include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's bulbs are typically dried and used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
